think_blocks:
The user wants me to segment the video into 1-minute intervals and provide a transcript and summary for each.

1.  **Analyze the video length:** The video is 642.8 seconds long.
2.  **Determine segments:**
    *   Segment 1: 00:00 - 01:00
    *   Segment 2: 01:00 - 02:00
    *   Segment 3: 02:00 - 03:00
    *   Segment 4: 03:00 - 04:00
    *   Segment 5: 04:00 - 05:00
    *   Segment 6: 05:00 - 06:00
    *   Segment 7: 06:00 - 07:00
    *   Segment 8: 07:00 - 08:00
    *   Segment 9: 08:00 - 09:00
    *   Segment 10: 09:00 - 10:00
    *   Segment 11: 10:00 - 10:42.8

3.  **Process each segment:**

    *   **Segment 1 (00:00 - 01:00):**
        *   Content: Introduction of Sharon Choi, Bong Joon-ho praising her translation skills. Transition to BAFTA backstage interview. Bong talks about class differences and stairs.
        *   Transcript: "Yeah. She really destroyed the language barrier. Yeah. Thank you. 나라나 가난한 자와 부자들이 있고 그들 사이에 되게 가파른 계단이 있죠. 여기 로얄 엘버터홀에도 계단이 되게 많아서 땀이 막 날라 그러는데 I think in any country, you have the rich and you have the poor, and between those classes, you have very steep staircases. So today, going up and down the stairs of Royal Albert Hall, it's making me sweat."

    *   **Segment 2 (01:00 - 02:00):**
        *   Content: Bong explains the metaphor of stairs in Parasite. Transition to Memories of Murder retrospective. Song Kang-ho jokes about his role.
        *   Transcript: "이 패러사이트도 계단 영화예요. 스토리를 요약하면 계단을 올라가려 했던 한 가난한 남자가 계단을 오히려 내려가면서 끝나는 얘기인데 그것이 어떻게 보면 우리 지금 시대에 담고 있는 슬픈 모습이다. 이런 생각을 하면서 만들었죠. And Parasite is also a movie about staircases. To summarize the story, it's essentially about a poor man who wanted to go up the stairs but ends with going down the stairs. And I think it's a sad portrait of our current times. Did you see Versailles? 이 생중보다 훨씬 재미있는 영화입니다. 왜냐하면 제가 많이 나와요. This movie is so much better than Parasite because you will see more of me in this film. 이건 없이 볼 수 있습니다. You will be almost sick of me after this film. 많이 기대해 주십시오. 감사합니다."

    *   **Segment 3 (02:00 - 03:00):**
        *   Content: HCAA awards. Bong introduces Han Jin-won. Transition to NYFF panel. Bong explains the concept of family and eating together.
        *   Transcript: "그리고 저의 어떤 파트너로 같이 작업을 한 멋진 작가가 있는데 오늘 약간 외관상 변호사나 회계사의 모습이긴 하지만 실제로는 아주 And my great partner in writing is here, today he looks like a lawyer or an accountant, but he is always filled with very perverted great ideas. 한진원 It's fine, but the translator will repeat and translate. So she was surprised at how detailed the family was. relationships were in the in the film so she wants to know how he developed that ensemble I don't know. 그런 개념 자체에 좀 그래서 출발을 했죠. 같이 유난히 다 같이 먹는 장면이 많았잖아요. 우리가 바베큐도 해 먹고."

    *   **Segment 4 (03:00 - 04:00):**
        *   Content: Continuation of NYFF panel. Bong explains the Korean word for family and eating scenes. Transition to SBIFF Q&A. Bong compares budgets of Parasite vs. Snowpiercer/Okja.
        *   Transcript: "So in Korean, the word family is comprised of two Chinese characters that basically means to eat together. So that's where that whole chemistry began. You see a lot of scenes where they're eating together, they're grilling meat together and all that. 저의 바로 이전 작품이 옥자라는 영화인데 좀 약간 죄책감을 느끼지만 어쩔 수 없이 바베큐 장면을 많이 넣을 수밖에 없었고요. So since my previous one was Okja, I did feel guilty about including all those barbecue scenes, but I had to. 제 부모님은 혁신사회의 위원회의 위원회의 위원회의 위원회입니다. I'm very nervous here. 오피어서나 옥자에 비해서 예산도 잡고 영화도 장소 두 개에서 뭔가 더 집중된 영화지만 맞습니다. 주제적인 스토리적인 욕심은 더 있었죠. 말씀하신 게 맞는 것 같아요."

    *   **Segment 5 (04:00 - 05:00):**
        *   Content: SBIFF Q&A continues. Bong talks about ambition and the magnifying glass metaphor. Transition to Landmark Theatre interview. Bong discusses genre vs. social commentary.
        *   Transcript: "So compared to Snowpiercer and Okja, Parasite had a much smaller budget and the film is very focused on these two spaces. But in terms of the themes and story, I was more ambitious with this film. 어렸을 때 돋보기 가지고 햇빛을 모아서 종이를 태우면 연기가 나면서 타잖아요. 그때 작은 초점이 모이면서 그때 종이가 탈 때 어떤 쾌감 같은 그런 집중력을 만들어 보고 싶었어요. So when we were little, we would use magnifying glasses to burn very small dots onto the paper. And you would always see the smoke coming from the paper from this tiny dot, focus from the sunlight. And I really wanted to portray that sort of excitement and joy that comes from that tiny dot burning. 사실 뭐 어떤 정치적인 주제나 무슨 사회적인 코멘트를 하려고 영화를 만든 적은 없고요. 항상 장르적인 흥분 내지는 재미가 있는 영화를 인물들에 대해서 자꾸 이렇게 파내려가다 보면 왠지."

    *   **Segment 6 (05:00 - 06:00):**
        *   Content: Landmark Theatre interview continues. Bong explains how character exploration leads to social themes. Transition to SBIFF red carpet. Bong discusses revolution and complexity.
        *   Transcript: "So to be honest, I don't create films to deliver a particularly political message or provide social commentary. I really pursue the excitement and entertainment that comes from genre itself. But I always have a lot of interest in characters and individuals. 개인을 파내려가다 보니까 왠지 점점 파내려갈수록... 사회, 역사랑 저절로 연결이 되나 봐요. 이렇게 띌려야 뗄 수 없나 봐요. 제가 무인도에서 평생 사는 사람 영화를 찍지 않는 이상은. The deeper I delve into these individuals, the more I come to explore society and history. They're basically inseparable. And so unless I create a film about a man who lives on an isolated island, I think they will always go hand in hand. Song King Can we say that? 혁명, 오히려 혁명으로부터 점점 거리가 멀어지는 것 같아요, 세상이. 혁명의 시대가 많이 지나가고, 혁명이란 것은 뭔가 부서트려야 될 대상이 있어야 되는 것인데."

    *   **Segment 7 (06:00 - 07:00):**
        *   Content: SBIFF red carpet interview continues. Bong explains the complexity of modern society and how Parasite reflects that. Transition to SBIFF Q&A. Bong discusses genre conventions and Korean reality.
        *   Transcript: "그게 뭔지, 혁명을 통해 깨트려야 되는 게 뭔지 파악하기가 되게 힘들고 복잡한 세상이 되고 있는 것 같아요. 그런 복잡한 상황을 표현하는 것 같아요. 패러사이트는 오히려. I think the world is actually going farther away from revolution. We are, you know... Because revolution means that you have a target to fight against, something that you identify as a target to destroy, but we now live in a very complicated world where it's impossible to discern our opponents, and I think that's the complexity that Parasite is about. Yeah. 영화의 역사들이라든가 장르의 컨벤션 이런 것들을 되게 사랑하는데 또 동시에 저는 한국 감독이고 한국에 저를 둘러싼 어떤 현실들이 있어요. 제가 일상에서 관찰하는 것들이 있고 제가 장르의 컨벤션을 깨트리거나 어기거나 다른 길로 갈 때 그런 한국적인 공기나 현실 같은 게 쑥 들어오죠 그렇게 되면서 영화의 분위기가 아주 기묘해진달까요?"

    *   **Segment 8 (07:00 - 08:00):**
        *   Content: SBIFF Q&A continues. Bong explains how he blends genre conventions with Korean reality. Transition to Academy Conversations. Bong discusses the flood scene production.
        *   Transcript: "So I'm someone who loves film history and genre conventions but at the same time as a Korean director I'm very sensitive to the reality of Korea and I always observe what happens in my daily life set in Korea. So whenever I try to break or steer away from genre conventions I think I always reflect the air and the atmosphere that's specifically Korean and bring that to my films which creates that sort of strange and unique nuance to my film. 아니 뭐 실제 로케이션에 홍수를 나게 할 수는 없잖아요 We couldn't submerge an actual neighborhood underwater. 그래서 그 가난한 동네를 여기 이제 배우들 이분들 집과 그 가난한 동네를 다 그 수영장에, 풀장에, 워터탱크에 지은 거죠. So we built their home, the Kim family home, and all the homes surrounding that and that entire neighborhood. We built it all in a water tank."

    *   **Segment 9 (08:00 - 09:00):**
        *   Content: Academy Conversations continues. Transition to Golden Globe Awards. Bong gives his acceptance speech about subtitles.
        *   Transcript: "저는 포린 랭귀지 필름 메이커입니다. 여기가 전화권을 가지고 있습니다. 이해하시길 바랍니다. Thank you. 자막, 서브타이틀의 장벽을 장벽도 아니죠. 한 1인치 정도 되는 그 장벽을 뛰어넘으면 여러분들이 훨씬 더 많은 영화를 즐길 수 있습니다. Once you overcome the one inch tall barrier of subtitles, you will be introduced to so many more amazing films."

    *   **Segment 10 (09:00 - 10:00):**
        *   Content: Academy Awards Best Director speech. Bong quotes "The most personal is the most creative." Transition to press room. Reporter asks about Sharon Choi.
        *   Transcript: "그 어렸을 때 제가 항상 가슴에 새겼던 말이 있었는데 영화 공부할 때 가장 개인적인 것이 가장 창의적인 것이다. 그 말을 하셨던 분이 누구였냐면 책에서 읽은 거였지만 Thank you so much. When I was young and studying cinema, there was a saying that I carved deep into my heart, which is the most personal is the most creative. I don't know. all over the world war king Finish this war. bookmaker are you gonna miss her because stupid together for the whole journey You already know she's a filmmaker and she studied the film in the university. Thank you. Yeah."

    *   **Segment 11 (10:00 - 10:42.8):**
        *   Content: Press room interview concludes. Bong expresses interest in Sharon's script.
        *   Transcript: "So I'm so curious about her screen Actually, she's right. them. I am so curious about it."
